56.reason: [When the stack is empty, the pop() function does not return a value, leading to undefined behavior when the returned value is used in main(). This causes incorrect output or runtime issues.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.5 , reason:  [When the stack is empty, the pop() function does not return a value, leading to undefined behavior when the returned value is used in main(). This causes incorrect output or runtime issues.]

72.reason: [If pop() prints "error " due to an empty stack, it doesn't return a valid integer. Using the uninitialized/garbage return value leads to printing garbage instead of handling the error properly.]
